growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: fierer.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: MIMS Vmax (accelerated over 43 h from initial 0.64)

sec.method: produced during CH4 oxidation; not quantified per gDW in this batch condition

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: we used MIMS to determine Michaelis-Menten kinetics for CH4 and H2 oxidation in M. bryophila H2s T, which was pre-grown in batch culture without CH4 limitation (mu = 0.065 h-1). This resulted in a maximum CH4 oxidation rate of 159 umol min-
growth_records.notes: Batch, CH4-replete (Table 1 footnote c). Growth rate mu=0.065 h-1 reported. Doubling time td=ln2/0.065=10.66 h. Vmax CH4=159 umol/min/gDW = x0.06 = 9.54 mmol/gDW/h; Km(app) CH4=3.5 uM. Vmax H2 rose 0.64->14.2 umol/min/gDW over 43 h (=0.038->0.852 mmol/gDW/h); Km H2 n.d. Rates are MIMS-measured maximal substrate oxidation (=uptake) rates.

up.method: MIMS Vmax (accelerated from 8 to 20 over time; D2 tracer)

sec.method: GC gas outflow; CH4->CO2 conversion 55%+/-1% without H2, dropping to 13%+/-0.4% (n=5 d) during H2 supply

yields.Yxs: 6.9 g DW/mol CH4 (CH4 alone); 14 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2), adapted medium, D=0.006

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: When the bioreactor reached steady state and H2 was added, consumption started immediately and was 3-fold higher than the CH4 consumption. During H2 supply, the conversion of CH4 to CO2 dropped from 55%+/-1% to 13%+/-0.4% (n=5 days)... corr
growth_records.notes: CH4- and O2-limited chemostat, D=0.006 h-1 = mu at steady state; td=ln2/0.006=115.5 h. Vmax CH4=155 umol/min/gDW (x0.06=9.30 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) CH4=3 uM. Vmax H2 8->20 umol/min/gDW (0.48->1.20 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) H2=0.03-0.06 uM; Km(app) H2/D2 measured as 35+/-5 nM, rising to 55 nM on CH4 addition. Under stable H2-replete conditions H2 consumption was 3-fold CH4 consumption. Yield reassessed with adapted (NTA + extra Ni2+/Cu2+) medium: 6.9 (CH4 alone) -> 14 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2); OD600 0.94->1.83. DW: OD600=1 ~247.5+/-4.6 mg/L at D=0.006. Rates are MIMS Vmax = maximal substrate oxidation/uptake.

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: we reduced the dilution rate... first from D = 0.006 h-1 to D = 0.003 h-1, and then to D = 0.0016 h-1... This resulted in an almost threefold decrease in the maximum CH4 oxidation rate, which dropped from 155 to 61 umol min-1 g-1 DW
growth_records.notes: CH4/O2-limited chemostat D=0.003 h-1 = mu; td=ln2/0.003=231 h. Vmax CH4=84 umol/min/gDW (x0.06=5.04 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) CH4=2.5-3 uM (Table 1). Vmax H2 and Km H2 = n.d. at this D. This same D=0.003 culture was used for H2S inhibition assays: CH4 oxidation inhibited 48%/74%/85% by 0.7/1.4/2 uM H2S. No DW determined at D=0.003; authors used mean of D=0.0016 and 0.006 (271.6 mg DW/L) for Vmax calc.

growth_records.medium: Alphaproteobacterial methanotroph mineral salts medium (Methylocystis/Methylosinus), NTA-adapted trace elements

up.method: MIMS Vmax (accelerated from 4 to 24 over time)

sec.method: CO2 in gas outflow dropped upon H2 supply (Figure 4); not quantified per gDW

yields.Yxs: 5.8 g DW/mol CH4 (CH4 alone) -> 13.1 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2); 7.5 g DW/mol CH4 on CH4 alone after H2 removed

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: the latter operated at a dissolved CH4 concentration of 0.11 uM... the maximum CH4 oxidation rate, which dropped from 155 to 61 umol min-1 g-1 DW... The calculated yield of the culture without H2 compared to with H2 supply increased from 5.
growth_records.notes: Severe CH4/O2-limited chemostat D=0.0016 h-1 = mu; td=ln2/0.0016=433.2 h. Vmax CH4=61 umol/min/gDW (x0.06=3.66 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) CH4=1-2 uM. Vmax H2 4->24 umol/min/gDW (0.24->1.44 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) H2=0.15-0.20 uM. Under stable H2-replete conditions total H2 consumption exceeded CH4 consumption by factor 6-7. Yield: without H2 5.8 -> with H2 13.1 g DW/mol CH4; on CH4 alone after H2 removed 7.5 (culture was trace-element-limited during first period). DW: OD600=1 = 295.8+/-4.7 mg/L at D=0.0016. Rates are MIMS Vmax = uptake. NTA-adapted trace-element medium used during long-term H2 supply (base recipe otherwise identical).
